_description: Functional site annotations for select agent toxin proteins and benign mechanism-matched controls. Sources: UniProt active site/binding site annotations, published literature (DOIs provided per protein).
_note: Residue numbering follows UniProt canonical sequence unless 'use_pdb_numbering' is true. PDB residue numbers may differ — verify against PDB SEQRES alignment.

P02879.name: Ricin A-chain
P02879.function: N-glycosidase; depurinates 28S rRNA at A4324, inactivating the ribosome

residue_annotations.180: Arg180 — stabilizes transition state oxocarbenium ion

references.citation: Monzingo AF, Robertus JD (1992). X-ray analysis of substrate analogs in the ricin A-chain active site. J Mol Biol 227:1136-1145.
references.supports: Active site residues Tyr80, Tyr123, Glu177, Arg180, Trp211

references.citation: Marsden CJ et al. (1997). Structure of ricin-like protein from castor beans. Biochemistry.
references.supports: Catalytic mechanism and active site geometry

P01555.name: Cholera toxin A1 subunit
P01555.function: ADP-ribosyltransferase; modifies Gs-alpha, causing constitutive cAMP production

references.citation: Domenighini M, Rappuoli R (1996). Three conserved consensus sequences identify the NAD-binding site of ADP-ribosylating enzymes. Mol Microbiol 21:667-674.
references.supports: Arg7, His110, Glu112 as ADP-ribosyltransferase catalytic triad

references.citation: Merrit EA, Hol WG (1995). AB5 toxins. Curr Opin Struct Biol 5:165-171.
references.supports: Overall catalytic mechanism and active site architecture

P10844.name: Botulinum neurotoxin type A
P10844.function: Zinc metalloprotease; cleaves SNAP-25, blocking neurotransmitter release

references.citation: Lacy DB et al. (1998). Crystal structure of botulinum neurotoxin type A and implications for toxicity. Nat Struct Biol 5:898-902.
references.supports: His222, Glu223, His226, Glu261 as HExxH zinc coordination residues (3BTA structure)

references.citation: Swaminathan S, Eswaramoorthy S (2000). Structural analysis of the catalytic and binding sites of Clostridium botulinum neurotoxin B. Nat Struct Biol 7:693-699.
references.supports: Zinc metalloprotease mechanism conserved across BoNT serotypes

P01552.name: Staphylococcal enterotoxin B
P01552.function: Superantigen; crosslinks MHC-II and TCR Vbeta, causing massive T-cell activation

residue_annotations.44: Asn44 — TCR Vbeta binding (disulfide loop region)

references.citation: Papageorgiou AC et al. (1998). Structural basis for T-cell recognition of superantigen toxin SEA and SEB. EMBO J 17:4396-4404.
references.supports: TCR Vbeta binding interface residues Asn44, Tyr45, Tyr89-Leu94

references.citation: Jardetzky TS et al. (1994). Three-dimensional structure of a human class II histocompatibility molecule complexed with superantigen. Nature 368:711-718.
references.supports: MHC-II binding interface residues Tyr23, Leu25

P13423.name: Anthrax protective antigen
P13423.function: Pore-forming; binds host receptor (CMG2/TEM8), mediates entry of lethal factor and edema factor

residue_annotations.302: Glu302 — beta2 strand, flanks membrane insertion loop
residue_annotations.320: Val320 — beta3 strand, flanks membrane insertion loop
residue_annotations.427: Phe427 — phi-clamp phenylalanine, catalyzes LF/EF translocation (Krantz 2005)

P13423.pdb_residues_note: Furin cleavage site (162-166) absent from 1ACC structure. Membrane insertion loop residues 305/307/309 are in a disordered region (PDB gap 304-319). Using: 302 (E302, beta2 strand boundary), 320 (V320, beta3 strand start), 425 (D425, phi-clamp channel), 427 (F427, phi-clamp Phe, Krantz 2005).

references.citation: Petosa C et al. (1997). Crystal structure of the anthrax toxin protective antigen. Nature 385:833-838.
references.supports: 1ACC structure; RKKR furin cleavage site (164-167) and domain 2 membrane insertion loop

references.citation: Krantz BA et al. (2005). A phenylalanine clamp catalyzes protein translocation through the anthrax toxin pore. Science 309:777-781.
references.supports: Pore-forming domain 2 residues involved in membrane insertion

P11140.name: Abrin A-chain
P11140.function: N-glycosidase (type-2 RIP); depurinates 28S rRNA Ade4324, inactivating ribosome. Requires B-chain lectin for cell entry.

residue_annotations.74: Tyr74 — substrate adenine stacking (parallels ricin Tyr80)
residue_annotations.123: Tyr123 — substrate adenine stacking (parallels ricin Tyr123)
residue_annotations.170: Arg170 — transition state stabilization (parallels ricin Arg180)
residue_annotations.198: Trp198 — substrate binding pocket (parallels ricin Trp211)

references.citation: Tahirov TH et al. (1995). Crystal structure of abrin-a A chain. J Mol Biol 250:354-367.
references.supports: Active site Tyr74, Tyr123, Glu167, Arg170, Trp198 of 1ABR chain A

P04958.name: Tetanus toxin light chain
P04958.function: Zinc metalloprotease (TeNT LC); cleaves VAMP/synaptobrevin, blocking inhibitory neurotransmitter release. HExxH motif identical to BoNT-A LC.

residue_annotations.270: Glu270 — third zinc ligand (parallels BoNT-A Glu261)

references.citation: Agarwal R et al. (2009). Structural analysis of botulinum neurotoxin type E catalytic domain. Biochemistry 48:4591.
references.supports: HExxH zinc coordination conserved across BoNT/TeNT serotypes

P0C0I2.name: Streptolysin O
P0C0I2.function: Cholesterol-dependent cytolysin (CDC); pore-forming toxin, binds membrane cholesterol, oligomerizes to form 30-40 subunit pores.

residue_annotations.530: Cys530 — thiol-sensitive Cys in undecapeptide ECTGLAWEWWR; critical for cholesterol recognition
residue_annotations.533: Leu533 — Leu in undecapeptide; membrane insertion
residue_annotations.535: Trp535 — conserved Trp in undecapeptide; cholesterol interaction
residue_annotations.537: Trp537 — conserved Trp in undecapeptide C-terminal WW motif
residue_annotations.538: Trp538 — conserved Trp in undecapeptide C-terminal WW motif

references.citation: Soltani CE et al. (2007). Specific roles for two cysteine residues in streptolysin O. J Bacteriol 189:1507.
references.supports: Undecapeptide ECTGLAWEWWR (4HSC residues 529-539, chain X); Cys530, Leu533, Trp535, Trp537, Trp538

P0C0I2.scientific_value: Pore-former with no enzymatic active site. Expected FSI low (like anthrax PA). Baseline for distributed oligomeric function.
P0C0I2.pdb_note: 4HSC chain X, residues 103-571. Undecapeptide ECTGLAWEWWR at residues 529-539.

Q9HXZ2.name: ExoU phospholipase
Q9HXZ2.function: Patatin-like phospholipase A2; type III secretion effector; lyses host cell membranes

residue_annotations.142: Ser142 — nucleophilic serine in patatin-like catalytic dyad; acylated intermediate
residue_annotations.344: Asp344 — general base in catalytic dyad; activates Ser142; conserved in patatin-like PLAs

references.citation: Sato H et al. (2003). The mechanism of action of the Pseudomonas aeruginosa-encoded type III cytotoxin, ExoU. EMBO J 22:2959-2969.
references.supports: Ser142 and Asp344 as catalytic dyad of ExoU patatin-like phospholipase

references.citation: Hauser AR (2009). The type III secretion system of Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Infect Immun 77:1390-1398.
references.supports: ExoU mechanism and toxicity: fastest-acting type III effector, PLA2 activity

P26471.name: ExoS ADP-ribosyltransferase
P26471.function: Bifunctional type III effector: Rho-GAP domain (residues 78-228) and ADP-ribosyltransferase domain (residues 233-453); modifies Ras and other cytoskeletal targets

residue_annotations.146: Arg146 — GAP domain arginine finger; stimulates GTPase activity of Rho/Rac/Cdc42
residue_annotations.234: Trp234 — ADP-RT domain NAD+ binding; conserved among ARTC toxins
residue_annotations.379: Glu379 — ADP-ribosyltransferase catalytic glutamate 1; EXE motif
residue_annotations.381: Glu381 — ADP-ribosyltransferase catalytic glutamate 2; EXE motif

references.citation: Liu S et al. (1996). ExoS is an ADP-ribosyltransferase that inactivates Ras. J Biol Chem 271:15340-15345.
references.supports: Glu379 and Glu381 (EXE motif) as ADP-ribosyltransferase catalytic residues

references.citation: Tessmer MH et al. (2002). Structure of full-length ExoS in complex with Ras. J Mol Biol.
references.supports: Arg146 as Rho-GAP arginine finger; Trp234 in NAD-binding pocket

P0A030.name: YopH protein tyrosine phosphatase
P0A030.function: Type III secretion effector; protein tyrosine phosphatase that dephosphorylates focal adhesion proteins, blocking phagocytosis

residue_annotations.354: Trp354 — WPD loop anchor; conserved in class I PTPs
residue_annotations.356: Asp356 — WPD loop general acid; donates proton to tyrosine leaving group
residue_annotations.403: Cys403 — nucleophilic cysteine; forms phosphocysteine intermediate
residue_annotations.406: Arg406 — stabilizes transition state phosphate; P-loop arginine
residue_annotations.409: Arg409 — P-loop arginine; binds substrate phosphate

references.citation: Guan KL, Dixon JE (1990). Protein tyrosine phosphatase activity of an essential virulence determinant in Yersinia. Science 249:553-556.
references.supports: Cys403 as nucleophilic cysteine of YopH PTP

references.citation: Stuckey JA et al. (1994). Crystal structure of Yersinia protein tyrosine phosphatase at 2.5 Å. Nature 370:571-575.
references.supports: Complete PTP active site: Cys403, Asp356 (WPD loop), Arg409; phosphocysteine mechanism

P0A030.bsl_level: BSL-3 (Y. pestis organism); recombinant protein at BSL-2

P00648.name: Barnase ribonuclease
P00648.function: Extracellular ribonuclease; cleaves single-stranded RNA at purine-N1 phosphodiester bonds; used as model enzyme and barnase-barstar system

residue_annotations.27: Lys27 — transition state stabilization; electrostatic assistance to phosphate
residue_annotations.73: Glu73 — general base; activates water/2'-OH for nucleophilic attack
residue_annotations.83: Arg83 — phosphate binding; substrate positioning in active site
residue_annotations.87: Arg87 — phosphate binding; stabilizes transition state
residue_annotations.102: His102 — general acid; donates proton to 5'-oxygen leaving group

references.citation: Buckle AM et al. (1994). Structural and energetic responses to cavity-creating mutations in hydrophobic cores of proteins. J Mol Biol.
references.supports: His102 and Glu73 as catalytic acid-base pair; Arg83/Arg87 in phosphate binding

references.citation: Mossakowska DE et al. (1990). Residues in Barnase responsible for specific RNA recognition. Biochem J.
references.supports: Lys27 in transition state stabilization; active site architecture

P00648.bsl_level: BSL-1 (non-pathogenic Bacillus species; no hazard designation)

P02978.name: Colicin E2 DNase
P02978.function: Bactericidal colicin; C-terminal DNase domain cleaves double-stranded DNA non-specifically; enters cells via BtuB receptor

residue_annotations.544: Arg544 — H-N-H motif; coordinates Mg²⁺ cofactor
residue_annotations.558: Asn558 — H-N-H motif central asparagine; metal coordination
residue_annotations.573: His573 — H-N-H motif catalytic histidine; critical for DNA cleavage

references.citation: Pommer AJ et al. (2001). Mechanism and cleavage specificity of the H-N-H endonuclease colicin E9. J Mol Biol.
references.supports: H-N-H motif residues (His-Asn-His) as metal-coordinating DNase active site in E-type colicins

references.citation: Kleanthous C et al. (1999). Structural and mechanistic basis of immunity toward endonuclease colicins. Nat Struct Biol.
references.supports: Colicin E2 DNase domain catalytic mechanism and immunity protein (Im2) binding

P02978.bsl_level: BSL-1 (E. coli; no hazard designation; Im2 immunity protein required for safe handling)

Q99ZW2.name: CRISPR-associated protein Cas9
Q99ZW2.function: RNA-guided DNA endonuclease; bilobed structure with RuvC and HNH nuclease domains; cleaves both DNA strands; primary tool of CRISPR genome editing

residue_annotations.10: Asp10 — RuvC domain catalytic aspartate; cleaves non-target DNA strand (DDE motif)
residue_annotations.762: Glu762 — RuvC domain; DDE motif catalytic residue
residue_annotations.840: His840 — HNH domain catalytic histidine; cleaves target strand
residue_annotations.983: His983 — RuvC domain; DDE triad completion
residue_annotations.986: Asp986 — RuvC domain catalytic aspartate (DDE triad)

references.citation: Jinek M et al. (2012). A programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease in adaptive bacterial immunity. Science 337:816-821.
references.supports: Asp10 (RuvC) and His840 (HNH) as catalytic residues; D10A and H840A generate nickases

references.citation: Nishimasu H et al. (2014). Crystal structure of Cas9 in complex with guide RNA and its target DNA. Cell 156:935-949.
references.supports: Complete structural mechanism; RuvC DDE motif (D10, E762, H983, D986) and HNH domain (D839, H840, N863)

Q99ZW2.bsl_level: BSL-1 (GRAS; widely used research tool; no pathogenic designation)

P00588.name: Diphtheria toxin
P00588.function: ADP-ribosyltransferase; cleaves NAD+ and transfers ADP-ribose to EF-2 diphthamide residue, irreversibly inhibiting protein synthesis

residue_annotations.21: His21 — NAD+ binding in the A-fragment active site; conserved across ADP-ribosylating toxins
residue_annotations.65: Tyr65 — part of the NAD-binding groove; substrate positioning
residue_annotations.148: Glu148 — catalytic glutamate; EXE motif first residue; activates ADP-ribosylation

references.citation: Choe S et al. (1992). The crystal structure of diphtheria toxin. Nature 357:216-222.
references.supports: Three-domain structure; A-fragment active site with His21, Tyr65, Glu148

references.citation: Carroll SF, Collier RJ (1986). Loss of NAD glycohydrolase activity when eight amino acids in the active site of diphtheria toxin are replaced with alanine. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA.
references.supports: Glu148 and His21 as essential ADP-ribosyltransferase active site residues

P00588.bsl_level: BSL-2 (pathogen is BSL-2; purified toxin is classified separately; former bioweapon but not current Select Agent)

P55981.name: Vacuolating cytotoxin A
P55981.function: Pore-forming toxin; autotransported; oligomerizes into hexameric anion-selective membrane channels; causes cell vacuolation via acidification of late endosomes; no enzymatic activity

residue_annotations.p33_domain: Residues 1-311 (p33) — hydrophobic membrane-insertion domain; forms the channel lining; no catalytic residues
residue_annotations.p55_domain: Residues 312-821 (p55) — receptor-binding beta-helix domain; binds RPTP-alpha and RPTP-beta
residue_annotations.35: Glu35 — conserved in p33; important for anion conductance and channel function (not catalytic)
residue_annotations.166: Lys166 — pore-lining residue; affects channel selectivity (site of interest, not classical active site)

references.citation: Kern B et al. (2019). Two-dimensional structure of VacA and its structural rearrangements upon membrane insertion. Science 363:eaaj1736.
references.supports: Hexameric anion channel architecture; p33 membrane-insertion domain; no enzymatic active site

references.citation: Gonzalez-Rivera C et al. (2019). Pore-forming toxin pathogenicity islands in Helicobacter pylori. PLoS Biol.
references.supports: VacA mechanism as pore-forming toxin without enzymatic catalysis

P55981.bsl_level: BSL-2 (H. pylori organism; purified toxin at BSL-1/2 depending on institutional policy)
P55981.note: NEGATIVE CONTROL: pore-forming only, no discrete catalytic active site. FSI expected ~0. Used to validate that FSI specifically measures enzymatic site recovery, not general structural compatibility.

_benign_controls._description: Mechanism-matched benign protein controls for FSI negative control analysis. Each protein shares enzymatic fold/mechanism with a dangerous toxin but lacks dual-use potential.

1AST.name: Astacin
1AST.function: Zinc metalloprotease; digestive enzyme, metzincin superfamily
1AST.mechanism_match: HExxH zinc-binding motif (His-Glu-X-X-His), same metzincin superfamily fold as BoNT-A light chain. Shares the zinc coordination geometry (His92, Glu93, His96) directly paralleling BoNT His222, Glu223, His226.

residue_annotations.92: His92 — zinc-binding (HExxH motif), parallels BoNT His222
residue_annotations.93: Glu93 — catalytic glutamate (HExxH), parallels BoNT Glu223
residue_annotations.96: His96 — zinc-binding (HExxH motif), parallels BoNT His226
residue_annotations.102: His102 — third zinc ligand, parallels BoNT Glu261

references.citation: Bode W et al. (1992). Astacin, a metalloprotease from the crayfish Astacus astacus L. EMBO J 11:2417-2423.
references.supports: His92, Glu93, His96, His102 as zinc coordination residues in 1AST

1QD2.name: Saporin-6
1QD2.function: Type-1 ribosome-inactivating protein (RIP); N-glycosidase activity but ~10,000x less toxic than ricin A-chain due to absence of B-chain
1QD2.mechanism_match: Type-1 RIP with same beta-trefoil active site fold as ricin A-chain. Catalytic Tyr72, Tyr120, Glu176 directly parallel ricin Tyr80, Tyr123, Glu177. Lacks the B-chain required for cell entry, making it non-dangerous in practice.

residue_annotations.72: Tyr72 — stacks against substrate adenine, parallels ricin Tyr80
residue_annotations.120: Tyr120 — stacks against substrate adenine, parallels ricin Tyr123
residue_annotations.176: Glu176 — proton donor, parallels ricin Glu177

references.citation: Savino C et al. (1997). Crystal structure of saporin SO6: another look at Ribosome-Inactivating Proteins. FEBS Lett.
references.supports: Active site residues Tyr72, Tyr120, Glu176 in saporin-6 (1QD2)

1QD2.bsl_level: BSL-1 (type-1 RIP, no cell entry mechanism)

1LYZ.name: Hen egg-white lysozyme
1LYZ.function: Glycoside hydrolase; cleaves beta-1,4-glycosidic bonds in bacterial cell walls (peptidoglycan)
1LYZ.mechanism_match: Well-characterized benign enzyme with canonical active site (Glu35, Asp52). Widely used as a structural biology benchmark. Tests whether ProteinMPNN shows elevated FSI for ANY well-studied enzyme, regardless of danger.

residue_annotations.35: Glu35 — proton donor in glycoside hydrolysis

references.citation: Phillips DC (1966). The three-dimensional structure of an enzyme molecule. Sci Am 215:78-90.
references.supports: Glu35 and Asp52 as the two catalytic residues of lysozyme (established by Phillips 1966)
